description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve the nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: After a server failover, the new MAC address is not updated on the remote VTEP VSX secondary node. Scenario: During a server failover, the remote VTEP VSX primary node correctly updates with the new MAC address. However, the VSX secondary node retains the previous MAC address, leading to an inconsistency between the two nodes. Workaround: Run clear arp.

description: Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

description: Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.
symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX.
scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features.
workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

messages.content: For 8100 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?

IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: A higher neighbor propagation delay will be observed when port speed is set to 2.5G. As a result, generalized Precision Time Protocol (gPTP) capability will become False and clients will not come up. Scenario: This issue can occur while changing port speeds to 2.5G on a 8360 switch using gPTP. Workaround: Use 1G/5G/10G port speeds instead.

description: Symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails. Scenario: Downloading/uploading the software Image via sm ubuntu IPv6 TFTP server fails. Workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1
symptom: TFTP Software image upload/download transfer operation fails.

workaround: Use the blocksize option in the copy command with a blocksize of 1375 or less. For example: Copy copy tftp://[20:1::100];blocksize=1375/image.swi secondary vrf vrf1

description: Symptom: The precision time protocol (PTP) offset is not accurate. Scenario: When configuring transceivers to a non-default forward error correction (FEC) setting, the PTP offset will not be accurate due to an inaccuracy in PTP latency. Workaround: Use the default FEC.
symptom: The precision time protocol (PTP) offset is not accurate.
scenario: When configuring transceivers to a non-default forward error correction (FEC) setting, the PTP offset will not be accurate due to an inaccuracy in PTP latency.
workaround: Use the default FEC.

description: Symptom: Extended RTs (via ext communities) received from a local side PE are not installed on the remote side PE. Scenario: In an L3VPN network, the extended RTs that are used as route attributes in the VPNv4 routes will not get installed on the other side PE. If those values are used to influence the route selection or policy, it may not work. Workaround: Use standard communities, other BGP attributes locally on the remote side to influence the route selection or policy.

description: Symptom: When switching between IPTV channels, there is an intermittent delay of 2-6 seconds before the new video stream is displayed to the end user. Scenario: The system experiences a small delay in the program of a new multicast flow after receiving the IGMP report. During this time, critical initial video packets containing SPS (Sequence Parameter Set) and PPS (Picture Parameter Set) information may be dropped. These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream. Workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

workaround: Configure static IGMP groups for the multicast streams as follows: static igmp join <multicast-group-address> interface <decoder-port> This ensures the hardware is pre-programmed with the multicast flows, eliminating the join latency. configure interface IP and Active gateway IP/MAC when interface is UP and running.

These packets are essential for the decoder to properly display the H.264 video stream..

description: Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.
